message:
feat(io): implement NPY binary format read/write with version and pickle policy support

Add complete NPY file format serialization and deserialization to
fnp-io, enabling round-trip encoding of typed NumPy arrays.

Writer (write_npy_bytes / write_npy_bytes_with_version):
- Encodes NpyHeader into Python dictionary literal format with correct
  padding to 16-byte alignment as required by the NPY spec.
- Supports version 1.0 (u16 header length), 2.0 and 3.0 (u32 header
  length) with appropriate preamble encoding.
- Validates payload byte count against shape * item_size for typed
  dtypes; object dtype requires explicit pickle marker (0x80 prefix).
- Enforces pickle policy gating: object dtype payloads are rejected
  unless allow_pickle=true.

Reader (read_npy_bytes):
- Parses NPY preamble, validates magic bytes and version, extracts
  header span with bounded budget (MAX_HEADER_BYTES).
- Custom Python dictionary parser handles descr/fortran_order/shape
  keys with strict key-set validation (rejects extra or missing keys,
  duplicate keys).
- Shape tuple parser enforces trailing-comma requirement for singleton
  tuples per Python/NumPy convention.
- Object dtype payloads validated for pickle marker presence and
  zero-sized consistency.

New public type NpyArrayBytes bundles version, parsed header, and raw
payload bytes for downstream consumption.

Test coverage includes: round-trip preservation, item-size misalignment
rejection, payload count mismatch detection, truncated header handling,
object dtype pickle policy gating on both read and write paths, v2
round-trip, extra-key rejection, singleton-without-comma rejection, and
zero-sized object payload edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
